Video.summary: Capt Justin & Meredith’s beautiful Panama City Beach morning is met with a slack tide so a new path to redfish success is found under a popular bridge.
Video.title: "A Bridge Too Red"

Video.summary: If you had a blank check to build the best fishing boat possible, what would you create? Capt. Justin gets his wish, but it doesn’t come quickly or easily.
Video.title: "Dream Boat Build"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin takes a fellow guide on his new boat to see how it measures up. Using only public waypoints on his Humminbird unit, they find exploring to be as much fun as it is fruitful.
Video.title: "Fishing the Dream Boat"

Video.summary: Capt. Justin heads offshore, dodges storms and shows how the "Return 'Em Right" program is saving fish that experience barotrauma.
Video.title: "Happy Returns"

Video.summary: This week, we reflect on a tragic and sad memory in our lives as we remember a dear childhood friend of my son Nick, Nathan Wimsatt. Our families have been close for man years and shared a lot of time together around the soccer field, vacations, fishing trips, and much more. Nathan passed in a tragic car accident at only 18 years on April 29th, 2023. This week his Dad Brian is joining me on Lake Taylorsville where we'll share some memories and celebrate Nathan's life...that ended way to soon!
Video.title: "Life Is A Highway"

Video.summary: Huckberry's flagship adventure series is back in Japan and the DIRT crew is hungry as ever for adventure. This time, they’re diving into the Kansai region—where millennia-old traditions flow from the shore of Lake Biwa to surf breaks on the coast. Chef Coleman Griffin gives them a taste of the deep cultural immersion that inspires his culinary practice, from chef knives descended from samurai swords to fermented carp that went on to spawn sushi. And turns out, the perfect meal does exist, and the crew knew it as soon as they had a bite of their final omakase.
Video.title: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"
DIRT - "Japanese Sushi Origins, Samurai Steel & Rich Culinary Traditions"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger meet up with Capt. Brain Wilson in Galveston, TX for a taste of what the Texas Gulf Coast has to offer.
Video.title: "Welcome to Galveston"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger join Capt. Brain Wilson in Galveston, TX for some hot Red Snapper action.
Video.title: "Red Snapper Slam"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger go north for a change to sight fish tripletails in Florida Bay.
Video.title: "Triple Tail in the Florida Bay"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger head to the hump on a windy day to target tunas and find some bonus mahis and tripletails as well.
Video.title: "Rough Day at the Hump"

Video.summary: Being consistently productive on the water means knowing how to adapt to conditions. Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger switch it up and fish shallow for sailfish.
Video.title: "Sailfish Save The Day"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger launch from the incomparable Oceans Edge Resort in Key West, Florida to hunt down all kinds of sport fish.
Video.title: "Key West Mahi and Sails"

Video.summary: What starts as a normal trip turns wild as Capt. Jared and Capt. Garrett get swarmed by Cobia.
Video.title: "The Prodigal Son"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared makes his way to Bolivar, Texas with Clay Guest & Ryan Pyburn.
Video.title: "Texas Mo Joe"

Video.summary: CA revisits some of the best moments of the year and some never before seen footage!
Video.title: "Bonus Round"

Video.summary: C.A. heads to Murrells Inlet, SC for some low country lure slingin'. What's on your menu? Shrimp and grits or Flounder?
Video.title: "Flounder Popsicle"

Video.summary: The Local Knowledge crew heads to Catalina Island off the coast of Southern California to participate in the 2024 War Heroes On Water tournament. This event offers a unique opportunity for the sport fishing community to create meaningful connections with our nation's heroes while raising awareness of the challenges they sometimes face when transitioning back to civilian life. With a little bit of luck and a lot of teamwork, Ali and Rush hope to put their veterans on a Bluefin tuna bite they won't forget!
Video.title: "War Heroes On Water"

Video.summary: Rush takes up an invite from good friend and fellow guide Jared Cyr to sight fish for barracuda and bonefish in the shallow waters of the Florida Keys. Meanwhile Rush's boat takes a break from charter fishing to get a new Seakeeper RIDE system installed.
Video.title: "Flats Ride"

Video.summary: Captains Jonathan Moss and Chris Shultz head offshore out of Mayport, Florida in search of massive Red Snapper!
Video.title: "Mayport Mayhem"

Video.summary: In Episode 2: New Orleans, Josh links up with Mason Hereford (owner of award-winning sandwich shop Turkey and the Wolf) and then hits the road for some epic hog hunting, swamp fishing, crawfish and oyster harvesting, and exploring Louisiana’s only water buffalo dairy farm. It all culminates in one incredible feast featuring unique regional dishes prepared by Mason with the help of some local friends.
Video.title: DIRT - "Louisiana's Wild Food & Award-Winning Cuisine | Hog Hunting, Swamp Fishing & MORE"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Louisiana's Wild Food & Award-Winning Cuisine | Hog Hunting, Swamp Fishing & MORE"
DIRT - "Louisiana's Wild Food & Award-Winning Cuisine | Hog Hunting, Swamp Fishing & MORE"
DIRT - "Louisiana's Wild Food & Award-Winning Cuisine | Hog Hunting, Swamp Fishing & MORE"

Video.summary: If you’ve ever heard, seen, or dreamed of the term van life, there is one man to thank for that, and his name is Foster Huntington. The filmmaker, photographer, and author has a storied career that includes the legendary book, "Home is Where You Park it". We met up with Foster (out on the road naturally) and he gave us a tour of his personal off-road camping rig he takes around the country.
Video.title: "How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"

Image.alt: "How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"
"How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"
"How this Van Life Pioneer Set Up His Perfect Off-Grid Camper | Foster Huntington"

Video.summary: Northern California is known for two things: its vast bodies of water and the potential to tap into some pure California gold in the form of bass. Whether it’s competitive tournament bass fishing or just being out for pure, unadulterated fun, Austin Bonjour has a passion like no other and stops at nothing to fulfill his drive to rise in the ranks of competitive bass fishing. You may have seen his name atop the leaderboards of the Apex Tour, Wild West Bass, or WON Bass, but this short film gives you a deeper look into the glorious yet driven mind of one of the West Coasts fishiest bass heads.
Video.title: "Blue Collar Bass Head - Austin Bonjour"

Video.summary: CA heads to the northern regions of the Nature Coast for a multi-species day.
Video.title: "Pressure Makes Diamonds"

Video.summary: With human populations scattered through the landscape, human-wildlife conflict is a common occurrence in Borneo. Dan Cabela travels to the area to participate in an ambitious elephant collaring project, hoping to better understand how people and elephants can live harmoniously.
Video.title: "Human-wildlife conflict in Borneo"

Video.summary: A remnant of the last Ice Age, the musk ox is almost mythical in appearance. For the people who share the Barren Ground region of Canada with them, they are an integral part of their culture. Show host, Dan Cabela, participates in a scientific sampling project to monitor the - population's health, and get first-hand experience of the relationship between local people and this arctic icon.
Video.title: "Barren Ground Musk Ox"

Video.summary: In rural Wisconsin, we follow the Yackley family who has dedicated their lives to competitive shooting. The five-member crew is the most decorated family in shooting sports. Becky Yackley gives tips to new shooters and then teams up with Chris Keefer to review Winchester USA Ready.
Video.title: "The Yackley 5"

Video.summary: Over 70 years ago, John Olin took 600 acres of farmland joined by timber and turned it into a wildlife oasis in hopes of creating an ideal place to raise and train the best Labradors Retrievers in the country. He called it Nilo, which was also Olin’s model of conservation. Today, Nilo is still considered one of the most prestigious hunting and shooting preserves in the nation. This is the story of Nilo.
Video.title: "Nilo"

Video.summary: The story of the game changing 350 Legend cartridge is documented at deer camps across the midwest with the Keefer Brothers in Kansas, Mark and Terry Drury in Missouri, Melissa Bachman in Nebraska and Team Winchester back in Missouri.
Video.title: “The Birth of a Legend”

Video.summary: The collaboration story between Winchester and Browning to create the groundbreaking 6.8 Western is featured. Tag along on a mule deer hunt in Utah with Chris Keefer and Winchester’s Jason Gilbertson, then follow Casey Keefer to Colorado for elk and Nebraska for whitetail where the 6.8 Western is put to the test.
Video.title: “The Western Front”

Video.summary: Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. After years of perfecting the recipe behind the scenes, Winchester finally unveiled the new Longbeard TSS at a turkey camp in northern Michigan. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.
Video.title: "Longbeard Mitten Mayhem"

Video.summary: Nestled in the hills of Ohio just west of the Appalachians is a very special deer camp ripe with memories from deer season’s past. With the development of the 350 Legend straight-walled cartridge by Winchester in 2019, a world of opportunities now existed in states like Ohio and Michigan. In this episode, with the 350 Legend in hand, three mature whitetails meet their maker, from the same blind, over the course of three seasons.
Video.title: "Remembering A Legend"

Video.summary: It’s been 60 years since AA was introduced to the shooting sports world. Since that day, an immeasurable number of AA hulls have hit the ground and countless shooters have stood center stage raising a trophy where millions of dollars have been won. Professional and recreational shooters alike have trusted AA for six decades. In this episode, load up for stories of high school, collegiate competitors and the world’s best as they bust clays with the legendary shot shell. - GUESTS: Zach Keenbaum (Team Winchester) Desi Edmunds (Team Winchester) Kayla Wilgus (Team Winchester) Amy Cawley (Team Winchester) Anthony Matarese (Team Winchester)
Video.title: "Six Decades Of Dominance"

Video.summary: For the first time in history, the World English Sporting Clays Championship was held in the United States at the incredible M&M Hunting Preserve owned by the Matarese family in New Jersey. Watch as Winchester’s best shooters vie for Gold as they compete against over 2,500 shooters and 17 countries. We’ll also join the Kids and Clays foundation in Florida as they host an event that will most certainly touch your soul.
Video.title: "Clays With Purpose"

Video.summary: In this episode, we catch up with and old friend of ours, Carl Parks. Carl's son CJ was killed in a tragic accident 6 years ago and we're gonna spend a day of fishing and reflection and celebration of CJ's life; We spent time with Carl years ago not long after the accident and filmed and episode called "The Wake" and now want to catch up again to showcase Carl's faith and strength that's helped him with his journey in life after losing his son.
Video.title: "The Wake Continues"

Video.summary: In this episode, Mark is joined by Matt Ogle, a proffesional bowler that Mark has known since Matt was 10 years old. Mark has been close to their family and has Matt grow into the bowling pro he is today. Matt was raised to love what he does…bassin and bowling the same way his Dad loved doing both. Matt's Dad actually ran the NABA Tournament Trail in KY for many years; Matt won the 2019 PBA Doubles Championship and the 2023 PBA Championship.
Video.title: "Bassin' & Bowling"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared Raskob joins legendary Captains Donny Lange and Charlie Scoble offshore in Islamorada.
Video.title: "Tackle Center"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared Raskob joins his long time friend Capt. Jake Roth for a day of fishing in the Florida Keys.
Video.title: "Jake Roth"

Video.summary: Mitch Widom, Andy Yaffa, & Chris Hammaway join Capt. Jared Raskob in Los Cabos on the hunt for Striped Marlin Part 2.
Video.title: "Cabo Marlin Part 2"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger take off from the beautiful Ocean's Edge Marina in Key West, Florida.
Video.title: "Kings on Kites"

Video.summary: Capt. Scott Walker and Capt. Steve Rodger meet up with Brice Barr to head out into the beautiful waters of Gulf in search of giants.
Video.title: "Gulf With Brice Barr"

Video.summary: In this episode of Local Lines Brandon Branch fishes Ozello Florida.
Video.title: "Day Saver"

Video.summary: In this episode of Local Lines Television, Brandon is fishing with Steve Grant of Diamond Fishing Products.
Video.title: "Wahoo Shootout"

Video.summary: In Today's episode, Brandon is going to be fishing with Larry from Save Crystal River. They will be targeting Redfish, Trout, and Bass.
Video.title: "Kings Bay"

Video.summary: Today Capt. Brandon is fishing the big bend with Capt. Matt.
Video.title: "Horseshoe Beach"

Video.summary: In this episode, Capt. Danny leaves the boat at home, in pursuit of some land-based fishing! Joined by his camera man for a day-off on the beautiful beaches of Anna Maria Island; the two are looking to tug on some ghostly beach snook, a true summertime classic!
Video.title: "Beach Boys"

Video.summary: We highlight organizations and programs making a difference in the Florida Keys. Learn how they help protect and restore these vital ecosystems.
Video.title: "Groups working to restore our vital ecosystems"

Image.alt: "Groups working to restore our vital ecosystems"
"Groups working to restore our vital ecosystems"
"Groups working to restore our vital ecosystems"

Video.summary: In this episode, we will highlight what’s being done in the Florida Keys to protect and save the reefs. We will show innovative solutions researchers have created to protect the coral and showcase the recovery that’s taking place.
Video.title: "What’s being done to protect our reefs"

Image.alt: "What’s being done to protect our reefs"
"What’s being done to protect our reefs"
"What’s being done to protect our reefs"

Video.summary: In this episode we will dive the beautiful reefs and wrecks of Key Largo. We will showcase research that is being done in Key Largo to protect reefs worldwide.
Video.title: "Key Largo"

Video.summary: On this show we will explore the crystal-clear waters of Islamorada to experience all this area has to offer. We will highlight the critical roll sharks play in our eco system and what is being done to protect them.
Video.title: "Islamorada"

Video.summary: Watch the video to learn more about bonefish and tarpon connectivity and the importance of conservation at local and regional levels.
Video.title: "Bonefish And Tarpon Connectivity"
